blocked.meaning: Do not run until blockers are remediated.

manual_gate.meaning: Human program owner must approve before use; evidence is incomplete or maturity is too low for autonomous expansion.

pilot_guarded.meaning: Approved for a bounded pilot or existing active use, but not for broad scale without the listed next actions.

scale_ready.meaning: Ready for controlled enterprise expansion with standard change controls.

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: Agentic remediation expansion decisions are generated from enforceable controls, identity contracts, connector trust, red-team coverage, and evidence readiness.
default_questions_answered: Which workflows remain pilot-only and why?
Which controls prevent tool misuse or rogue agent behavior?
Which MCP namespaces are still pilot dependencies?
Which source artifacts prove the readiness decision?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Use the scorecard as the AI platform promotion gate before expanding a workflow to more repositories, teams, or agent hosts.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open readiness scoring, then sell hosted score monitoring, enterprise evidence ingestion, custom control weighting, workflow promotion gates, and trust-center exports.

intent: Turn SecurityRecipes assurance artifacts into an enterprise adoption decision system: every approved agentic remediation workflow receives a repeatable scale, pilot, gate, or block decision from control evidence rather than product intuition.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Security Engineering, Product Security, GRC, procurement security, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.enterprise_promise: Security teams can decide which agentic remediation workflows are safe to scale using generated evidence across workflow scope, MCP policy, connector trust, agent identity, adversarial evals, and audit readiness.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI programs are moving from pilots to enterprise rollout. Buyers need a machine-readable way to separate workflows that are safe to scale from workflows that should remain gated, especially as MCP connectors, agent identities, and tool-calling systems become production control surfaces.

residual_risks.risk: The scorecard proves readiness of the reference operating model, not live customer enforcement.
residual_risks.treatment: Bind the scorecard to runtime MCP gateway logs, source-host review events, and customer IAM controls before production expansion.

residual_risks.risk: Scores can be high while pilot connectors still depend on customer-specific hardening.
residual_risks.treatment: Treat pilot connector workflows as pilot_guarded until connector owners provide production evidence and promotion approval.

residual_risks.risk: A scale decision can become stale after model, agent host, MCP connector, or workflow changes.
residual_risks.treatment: Run the generator in CI and require scorecard review during workflow maturity changes and connector promotions.

next_30_days: Use scale_ready workflows as the first enterprise expansion cohort.
Keep pilot_guarded workflows behind explicit rollout cohorts and reviewer capacity limits.
Attach the scorecard to AI platform intake and procurement-security review.
next_90_days: Promote pilot MCP connectors that repeatedly pass red-team drills and runtime audit review.
Add customer runtime telemetry ingestion so score decisions include live enforcement evidence.
Expose readiness deltas in hosted MCP and trust-center surfaces.

score_dimensions.question: Is the workflow owned, scoped, gated, measurable, and stoppable?
score_dimensions.title: Workflow Control Plane

score_dimensions.question: Are tool calls denied by default and constrained to declared decisions, paths, and runtime attributes?
score_dimensions.title: MCP Gateway Policy

score_dimensions.question: Does every allowed agent class have a non-human identity contract with explicit denies and runtime revocation?
score_dimensions.title: Agent Identity and Delegation

score_dimensions.question: Are all MCP namespaces registered, trusted, and appropriate for production or pilot use?
score_dimensions.title: MCP Connector Trust

score_dimensions.question: Does the workflow have repeatable red-team drills for hostile tool and agentic inputs?
score_dimensions.title: Adversarial Evaluation

score_dimensions.question: Can buyers and auditors trace claims to source-controlled and runtime evidence?
score_dimensions.title: Evidence Chain

score_dimensions.question: Is the workflow mature enough to expand beyond a controlled pilot?
score_dimensions.title: Operational Maturity

standards_alignment.coverage: Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage functions for AI systems, including the April 2026 concept note direction for trustworthy AI in critical infrastructure.
standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0

standards_alignment.coverage: Generative AI risk actions for lifecycle governance, measurement, third-party dependencies, and data boundaries.
standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile

standards_alignment.coverage: Autonomous and agentic systems that plan, act, and make decisions across complex workflows.
standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026

standards_alignment.coverage: Confused deputy prevention, token passthrough avoidance, SSRF, session hijacking, local server compromise, and scope minimization.
standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices

standards_alignment.coverage: Secure defaults, transparency, executive ownership, and measurable security outcomes.
standards_alignment.name: CISA Secure by Design

standards_alignment.coverage: AI system component inventory, transparency, provenance, and risk-relevant dependencies.
standards_alignment.name: OWASP AI Bill of Materials Project
